title: Latvia Sole-Trader (Individual Merchant) Registration (Form KR2)
description: The Register of Enterprises' (Uzņēmumu reģistrs, UR) Form KR2 application for the entry of an individual merchant (individuālais komersants, Latvia's sole-trader form) into the Commercial Register ("Pieteikums individuālā komersanta ierakstīšanai komercreģistrā"). Opens Latvia as the registry's 75th jurisdiction (Business Formation, 1 of 6 verticals). This document models the individual merchant's own identity (a Latvian personal code, or for a person with none, date of birth, address, and identity-document details); the trade name (firma); the legal address decomposed to Latvia's own novads/pilsēta/pagasts/ciems address hierarchy plus a State Address Register cadastral designation; an optional micro-enterprise tax-payer status opt-in with its own attestation; an optional custodian/guardian block; an optional block for a representative signing on the merchant's behalf (reusing the same identity-block shape as the merchant's own); a free-text attached-documents list; contact information and a decision-notification-method choice; up to two signatures; and state-fee payment details. It excludes nothing beyond the applicant-facing fields themselves — the source form carries no separate clerk-only or "for official use" block. Filing occurs either on paper or through the Register of Enterprises' own service portal (registrs.ur.gov.lv); this schema does not file the application itself, and the live source is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Republic of Latvia or its Register of Enterprises.

authority.name: Register of Enterprises of the Republic of Latvia

source.documentRef: "KR2. VEIDLAPA — Pieteikums individuālā komersanta ierakstīšanai komercreģistrā", native .docx, docProps last-modified 2025-10-06

verification.notes:
GOV-4129 (child of GOV-4128, "GovSchema Standard Research"). Latvia's Business Formation
vertical — the Register of Enterprises' (Uzņēmumu reģistrs) Form KR2 sole-trader registration form — was banked
as a pre-scouted, ready-to-author candidate in the prior cycle (GOV-4121/GOV-4124, which opened Cyprus instead)
after Latvia's other five verticals were screened and found weaker: DMV (CSDD) is fully e.csdd.lv-login-gated
with no downloadable form; Visa is a confirmed duplicate of the EU-harmonized Schengen template already modelled
elsewhere in this registry; Passport and National ID (PMLP) are both in-person-appointment-only, with only a
stale 2017 third-party-hosted specimen found for either.

Re-fetched `https://apraksti.ur.gov.lv/lv/KR2%20veidlapa` fresh this cycle with a plain `curl` request (no
User-Agent gating, no login, no CAPTCHA): HTTP 200, `Content-Type:
application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`, `Content-Length: 47737`, served by a
Drupal 11 site (`x-generator: Drupal 11`). This is a native, unauthenticated `.docx` (Word Open XML), not a PDF —
`docProps/core.xml` records `dcterms:modified` of 2025-10-06T13:57:00Z, matching the pre-scouted estimate.
sha256: `9073145e1ba75a2bd51c90b6cb6d4af51c1d6d18666383a9eac6fc838f326595`.

## Structure of the source

The `.docx` carries no Word content-control (`w:sdt`) fields — every checkbox on the form is instead a literal
Wingdings glyph run (`<w:sym w:font="Wingdings" w:char="..."/>`) embedded directly in the text flow: `F0FD`
(three occurrences) marks an "Atzīmēt" ("Mark") instruction immediately preceding one or more selectable options,
and `F0A8` (five occurrences) is the empty checkbox glyph for each individual option — four real checkboxes
(custodian/guardian in Section 5; mail/email notification in Section 6) plus one decorative use directly before
the "Apliecinājums" sub-heading in Section 4 that is not itself a fillable option. All ten of the form's own
numbered sections were extracted from `word/document.xml`'s own paragraph/table-row structure (not OCR — this is
a born-digital document with a full, clean text layer), and every field below was verified against the raw XML,
not just the flattened text dump.

Three repeated identity sub-blocks — the individual merchant (Section 1), the appointed guardian (Section 5),
and the person signing on the merchant's behalf (Section 6) — each print the same two-header pattern
("Personas, kurai Latvijas Republikā ir piešķirts personas kods" vs. "...nav piešķirts") with no checkbox
distinguishing them; each is modelled with an invented `hasLatvianPersonalCode`-style gating boolean, the same
convention this registry uses elsewhere (e.g. `applicantIsMinor` in `cy/crmd/passport-application`) when a form
draws its own either/or line without a checkbox to encode it.

## Disclosed source-fidelity findings

1. **A structural asymmetry between the three identity sub-blocks.** Sections 1 and 5 both print an "Adrese, kurā
   persona ir sasniedzama" row under their "has a Latvian personal code" sub-header (with a "not obligatory
   unless no registered address" carve-out); Section 6's equivalent sub-header has no such address row at all —
   only "Personas kods." Modelled as printed (`signerHasLatvianPersonalCode` gates only `signerPersonalCode`,
   with no `signerAddress` counterpart), rather than assuming the omission was an oversight.
2. **The address-hierarchy rows in Section 3 (Novads/Novada pilsēta-pagasts/Pilsēta/Ciems) carry no per-row
   conditional marker.** Latvia's own administrative structure means only a subset of these rows applies to any
   one address (an independent city address uses "Pilsēta" alone; a rural address uses "Novads" plus "Ciems";
   etc.), but the form gives no field-level signal distinguishing which combination is in play. All four are
   modelled unconditionally optional, and the ambiguity is disclosed on each field rather than guessed at.
3. **Two distinct "not obligatory" carve-outs coexist for the same underlying concept.** The "has personal code"
   address row (Sections 1 and 5) carries an explicit conditional carve-out ("not obligatory unless no registered
   address"); the "no personal code" address row immediately below it carries none. Modelled as two separate
   fields (`address`/`guardianAddress` vs. `foreignAddress`/`guardianForeignAddress`) rather than one shared field,
   to avoid collapsing two differently-conditioned rows into a single requiredness rule.
4. **Section 10 (state-fee payment) is entirely conditionally skippable, with no field to gate on.** Its own
   header states the section is not filled in when the application is submitted via the Register of Enterprises'
   service portal (fee payable only through the portal) or when a separate payment-confirming document is
   attached — neither condition corresponds to a field anywhere on the form. Every Section 10 field is modelled
   unconditionally optional; the natural-person/legal-person payer split still uses an invented gating boolean
   (`stateFeePayerIsLegalPerson`, itself optional) mirroring the personal-code either/or convention.
5. **Section 9's two signature slots carry no role label.** Both are printed identically ("Vārds," "Uzvārds,"
   "Personas kods (ja nav personas koda, dzimšanas datums)," "Paraksts*," "Datums*") with nothing distinguishing
   whether the individual merchant, a guardian, or a representative belongs in the first slot versus the second —
   disclosed on `signatory2*` rather than guessed at.
6. **Section 4's confirmation text has no individual checkboxes of its own.** The "Apliecinājums" sub-heading is
   preceded by a decorative Wingdings glyph (the fifth `F0A8` occurrence, distinct from the four real checkboxes
   above), but the three "Apliecinu, ka:" bullet points beneath it are plain attestation text with no per-bullet
   checkbox — modelled as one combined `documents[]` attestation statement (`microEnterpriseTaxStatusAttestation`)
   rather than three separate boolean fields.

## Conformance

2 valid mock scenarios and 8 mutation-control fixtures committed under
`conformance/lv/ur/sole-trader-registration-kr2/1.0.0/`. An ephemeral, from-scratch mock validator (deriving
required/`requiredWhen`/`exclusivityGroups` rules directly from this schema's own `fields[]`/`documents[]`, not
committed) ran all 10 fixtures: both valid scenarios at 0 errors, all 8 mutation controls each raising exactly 1
error, and confirmed every `requiredWhen` field reference resolves (0 dangling references).

`node tools/validate.mjs` and `node tools/validate-ajv.mjs` both pass at 569/569 for the full registry,
individually and as part of the full run.

fields.name: givenName
fields.label: Vārds
fields.description: The individual merchant's (applicant's) given name.

fields.name: surname
fields.label: Uzvārds
fields.description: The individual merchant's surname.

fields.name: hasLatvianPersonalCode
fields.label: Personas, kurai Latvijas Republikā ir piešķirts personas kods
fields.description: Whether the individual merchant has been assigned a personal code (personas kods) in the Republic of Latvia. Not itself a printed checkbox — the form instead prints two alternate sub-headers ("Personas, kurai Latvijas Republikā ir piešķirts personas kods" vs. "...nav piešķirts") that gate two mutually exclusive field blocks. Modelled explicitly here, the same convention this registry uses when a form draws its own either/or line with no checkbox (e.g. `applicantIsMinor` in `cy/crmd/passport-application`).

fields.name: personalCode
fields.label: Personas kods
fields.description: The individual merchant's Latvian personal code (personas kods), format DDMMYY-XXXXX.
fields.sourceRef: Section 1, "Personas, kurai...ir piešķirts personas kods" block — "Personas kods"

fields.name: address
fields.label: Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama
fields.description: Address at which the individual merchant can be reached. The source form marks this "not obligatorily required, except if the person has no registered address" — i.e. required in practice only for a personal-code holder with no address of record in the Population Register, a condition with no corresponding field on the form. Left unconditionally optional rather than encoded.
fields.sourceRef: Section 1, "Personas, kurai...ir piešķirts personas kods" block — "Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama (nav norādāma obligāti, izņemot, ja personai nav reģistrēta adrese)"

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Dzimšanas datums
fields.description: The individual merchant's date of birth, required only for a person with no Latvian personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 1, "Personas, kurai...nav piešķirts personas kods" block — "Dzimšanas datums (diena/mēnesis/gads)"

fields.name: foreignAddress
fields.label: Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama
fields.description: Address at which the individual merchant can be reached, for a person with no Latvian personal code. Unlike `address` above, this row carries no "not obligatory" carve-out.
fields.sourceRef: Section 1, "Personas, kurai...nav piešķirts personas kods" block — "Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama"

fields.name: identityDocumentType
fields.label: Dokumenta veids
fields.description: The type of identity document held by the individual merchant (e.g. passport, national ID card), free text as the form provides no enumerated checkbox list.

fields.name: identityDocumentNumber
fields.label: Dokumenta nr.
fields.description: The individual merchant's identity document number.

fields.name: identityDocumentIssueDate
fields.label: Izdošanas datums
fields.description: Date the individual merchant's identity document was issued.

fields.name: identityDocumentIssuingCountry
fields.label: Izdevējs — Valsts
fields.description: Country that issued the individual merchant's identity document.

fields.name: identityDocumentIssuingInstitution
fields.label: Izdevējs — Institūcija
fields.description: Institution that issued the individual merchant's identity document.

fields.name: businessName
fields.label: Firma
fields.description: The individual merchant's trade name (firma) under which the business will be entered into the Commercial Register.

fields.name: legalAddressNovads
fields.label: Novads
fields.description: Municipality (novads) of the legal address, per Latvia's State Address Register (Valsts adrešu reģistrs). Latvia's address hierarchy means only a subset of the Novads/Novada pilsēta-pagasts/Pilsēta/Ciems rows apply to any one address depending on whether it is an independent city, a novads town/parish, or a rural village — the form gives no per-row conditional marker distinguishing which combination applies, so all four are modelled unconditionally optional.

fields.name: legalAddressNovadaPilsetaOrPagasts
fields.label: Novada pilsēta/pagasts
fields.description: Town or parish (pilsēta/pagasts) within the novads, of the legal address. See `legalAddressNovads` for the disclosed address-hierarchy ambiguity shared by this row.

fields.name: legalAddressPilseta
fields.label: Pilsēta
fields.description: City (pilsēta) of the legal address, for an address in an independent republican city rather than a novads. See `legalAddressNovads` for the disclosed address-hierarchy ambiguity shared by this row.

fields.name: legalAddressCiems
fields.label: Ciems
fields.description: Village (ciems) of the legal address, for a rural address within a pagasts. See `legalAddressNovads` for the disclosed address-hierarchy ambiguity shared by this row.

fields.name: legalAddressStreetName
fields.label: Ielas nosaukums
fields.description: Street name of the legal address, where the address is street-based.

fields.name: legalAddressHouseNameOrNumber
fields.label: Mājas nosaukums/mājas nr., korpuss
fields.description: House name (for a rural farmstead-style address) or house number and building/block (korpuss), for a street-based address.

fields.name: legalAddressApartmentNumber
fields.label: Dzīvokļa numurs
fields.description: Apartment number of the legal address, where applicable.

fields.name: legalAddressPostalCode
fields.label: Pasta indekss LV-
fields.description: Four-digit Latvian postal code of the legal address. "LV-" is the form's own printed static prefix; only the four digits following it are entered in the blank cell.

fields.name: legalAddressCadastralDesignation
fields.label: Nekustamā īpašuma objekta (ēkas, dzīvokļa īpašuma vai telpas) kadastra apzīmējums
fields.description: Cadastral designation of the real-estate object (building, apartment property, or premises) at the legal address — 14 digits for a building, 17 digits for an apartment property or premises, per the form's own footnotes. Left optional: the form marks no explicit requiredness for this row, and not every legal-address arrangement (e.g. a leased space) may have its own distinct cadastral designation to cite.
fields.sourceRef: Section 3 — "Nekustamā īpašuma objekta (ēkas*, dzīvokļa īpašuma vai telpas**) kadastra apzīmējums"

fields.name: microEnterpriseTaxStatusRequested
fields.label: Atzīmēt, ja individuālais komersants piesakās mikrouzņēmumu nodokļa maksātāja statusa iegūšanai
fields.description: Whether the individual merchant is applying, on this same form, for micro-enterprise tax payer status. Absent/false means status is not being requested.
fields.sourceRef: Section 4, "Mikrouzņēmumu nodokļa maksātāja statuss" — checkbox row

fields.name: custodianAppointed
fields.label: ja individuālajam komersantam ir iecelts aizgādnis
fields.description: Whether a custodian (aizgādnis) has been appointed for the individual merchant. Per the form's own note, no further information about the custodian needs to be provided when this box is marked.

fields.name: guardianAppointed
fields.label: ja individuālajam komersantam ir iecelts aizbildnis un norādīt ziņas par aizbildni
fields.description: Whether a guardian (aizbildnis) has been appointed for the individual merchant. When marked, the guardian's own identity details below become required.

fields.name: guardianGivenName
fields.label: Vārds
fields.description: The appointed guardian's given name.

fields.name: guardianSurname
fields.label: Uzvārds
fields.description: The appointed guardian's surname.

fields.name: guardianHasLatvianPersonalCode
fields.label: Personas, kurai Latvijas Republikā ir piešķirts personas kods
fields.description: Whether the appointed guardian has a Latvian personal code. Invented gating field mirroring `hasLatvianPersonalCode` above, for the same reason: the form draws this either/or line with two sub-headers and no checkbox.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5, guardian identity block — sub-header pair

fields.name: guardianPersonalCode
fields.label: Personas kods
fields.description: The appointed guardian's Latvian personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5, guardian identity block, "...ir piešķirts personas kods" — "Personas kods"

fields.name: guardianAddress
fields.label: Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama
fields.description: Address at which the appointed guardian can be reached. Carries the same "not obligatory unless no registered address" carve-out as `address` above.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5, guardian identity block, "...ir piešķirts personas kods" — "Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama (nav norādāma obligāti, izņemot, ja personai nav reģistrēta adrese)"

fields.name: guardianDateOfBirth
fields.label: Dzimšanas datums
fields.description: The appointed guardian's date of birth, required only for a guardian with no Latvian personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5, guardian identity block, "...nav piešķirts personas kods" — "Dzimšanas datums (diena/mēnesis/gads)"

fields.name: guardianForeignAddress
fields.label: Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama
fields.description: Address at which the appointed guardian can be reached, for a guardian with no Latvian personal code. No "not obligatory" carve-out on this row.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5, guardian identity block, "...nav piešķirts personas kods" — "Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama"

fields.name: guardianIdentityDocumentType
fields.label: Dokumenta veids
fields.description: Type of identity document held by the appointed guardian.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5, guardian identity block, "Personu apliecinoša dokumenta dati" — "Dokumenta veids"

fields.name: guardianIdentityDocumentNumber
fields.label: Dokumenta nr.
fields.description: The appointed guardian's identity document number.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5, guardian identity block, "Personu apliecinoša dokumenta dati" — "Dokumenta nr."

fields.name: guardianIdentityDocumentIssueDate
fields.label: Izdošanas datums
fields.description: Date the appointed guardian's identity document was issued.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5, guardian identity block, "Personu apliecinoša dokumenta dati" — "Izdošanas datums (diena/mēnesis/gads)"

fields.name: guardianIdentityDocumentIssuingCountry
fields.label: Izdevējs — Valsts
fields.description: Country that issued the appointed guardian's identity document.

fields.name: guardianIdentityDocumentIssuingInstitution
fields.label: Izdevējs — Institūcija
fields.description: Institution that issued the appointed guardian's identity document.

fields.name: signerGivenName
fields.label: Vārds
fields.description: Given name of the person signing the application on the individual merchant's behalf. This section is filled in only if someone other than the individual merchant signs, and the form's own note says it does not need to be filled in when Section 5 already names a guardian.

fields.name: signerSurname
fields.label: Uzvārds
fields.description: Surname of the person signing on the individual merchant's behalf.

fields.name: signerHasLatvianPersonalCode
fields.label: Personas, kurai Latvijas Republikā ir piešķirts personas kods
fields.description: Whether the signer has a Latvian personal code. Invented gating field, same convention as `hasLatvianPersonalCode`/`guardianHasLatvianPersonalCode` above. Unlike those two blocks, this block's "has personal code" sub-header has no accompanying address row on the form — a genuine structural asymmetry, disclosed rather than modelled around.
fields.sourceRef: Section 6, signer identity block — sub-header pair

fields.name: signerPersonalCode
fields.label: Personas kods
fields.description: The signer's Latvian personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 6, signer identity block, "...ir piešķirts personas kods" — "Personas kods"

fields.name: signerDateOfBirth
fields.label: Dzimšanas datums
fields.description: The signer's date of birth, required only for a signer with no Latvian personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 6, signer identity block, "...nav piešķirts personas kods" — "Dzimšanas datums (diena/mēnesis/gads)"

fields.name: signerForeignAddress
fields.label: Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama
fields.description: Address at which the signer can be reached, for a signer with no Latvian personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 6, signer identity block, "...nav piešķirts personas kods" — "Adrese, kurā persona ir sasniedzama"

fields.name: signerIdentityDocumentType
fields.label: Dokumenta veids
fields.description: Type of identity document held by the signer.
fields.sourceRef: Section 6, signer identity block, "Personu apliecinoša dokumenta dati" — "Dokumenta veids"

fields.name: signerIdentityDocumentNumber
fields.label: Dokumenta nr.
fields.description: The signer's identity document number.
fields.sourceRef: Section 6, signer identity block, "Personu apliecinoša dokumenta dati" — "Dokumenta nr."

fields.name: signerIdentityDocumentIssueDate
fields.label: Izdošanas datums
fields.description: Date the signer's identity document was issued.
fields.sourceRef: Section 6, signer identity block, "Personu apliecinoša dokumenta dati" — "Izdošanas datums (diena/mēnesis/gads)"

fields.name: signerIdentityDocumentIssuingCountry
fields.label: Izdevējs — Valsts
fields.description: Country that issued the signer's identity document.

fields.name: signerIdentityDocumentIssuingInstitution
fields.label: Izdevējs — Institūcija
fields.description: Institution that issued the signer's identity document.

fields.name: signerLegalBasisForRepresentation
fields.label: Uz kāda tiesiska pamata persona pārstāv dibinātāju
fields.description: The legal basis on which the signer represents the founder (individual merchant), e.g. a power of attorney.
fields.sourceRef: Section 6 — "Uz kāda tiesiska pamata persona pārstāv dibinātāju"

fields.name: attachedDocumentsList
fields.label: Pievienotie dokumenti (jānorāda dokuments, eksemplāru skaits, lapu skaits)
fields.description: Free-text list of documents attached to this application, each with its number of copies and number of pages, as instructed. The source provides a single blank area rather than a structured table with separate document/copies/pages columns, so this is modelled as one free-text field rather than a `documents[]` checklist.

fields.name: notifyByMail
fields.label: Nosūtot pa pastu uz juridisko adresi
fields.description: Whether the state notary's decision should be sent by post to the legal address, applicable only if no official electronic address (e-adrese) is activated (in which case the decision is sent there automatically instead).

fields.name: notifyByEmail
fields.label: Nosūtot elektronisko dokumentu uz e-pasta adresi
fields.description: Whether the state notary's decision should be sent as an electronic document, signed with a secure electronic signature and timestamp, to an e-mail address.

fields.name: decisionNotificationEmailAddress
fields.label: (norādīt e-pasta adresi)
fields.description: E-mail address the state notary's decision is sent to, when `notifyByEmail` is selected.
fields.sourceRef: Section 8, second checkbox option — "(norādīt e-pasta adresi)"

fields.name: contactEmailAddress
fields.label: E-pasta adrese
fields.description: General contact e-mail address for correspondence, separate from `decisionNotificationEmailAddress`. Explicitly marked not obligatory on the form.
fields.sourceRef: Section 8, "Kontaktinformācija saziņai (informācija nav norādāma obligāti)" — "E-pasta adrese"

fields.name: contactPhoneNumber
fields.label: Tālruņa numurs
fields.description: General contact telephone number. Explicitly marked not obligatory on the form.
fields.sourceRef: Section 8, "Kontaktinformācija saziņai (informācija nav norādāma obligāti)" — "Tālruņa numurs"

fields.name: signatory1Name
fields.label: Vārds
fields.description: Given name of the first signatory to the application.

fields.name: signatory1Surname
fields.label: Uzvārds
fields.description: Surname of the first signatory.

fields.name: signatory1PersonalCodeOrDateOfBirth
fields.label: Personas kods (ja nav personas koda, dzimšanas datums)
fields.description: The first signatory's Latvian personal code, or date of birth if they have no personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 9, first signature block — "Personas kods (ja nav personas koda, dzimšanas datums)"

fields.name: signatory1Signature
fields.label: Paraksts*
fields.description: The first signatory's printed name, standing in for their physical or electronic signature. The form's own footnote (marked with an asterisk) says this does not need to be filled in when the document is signed with a secure electronic signature bearing a qualified timestamp — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, disclosed rather than encoded.

fields.name: signatory1Date
fields.label: Datums*
fields.description: Date the first signatory signed the application. The form's own footnote (marked with an asterisk) says this does not need to be filled in when the document is signed with a secure electronic signature bearing a qualified timestamp — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, disclosed rather than encoded.

fields.name: signatory2Name
fields.label: Vārds
fields.description: Given name of a second signatory to the application, where the form's own two generic signature slots are both used. The form does not label which role (the individual merchant, a guardian, or a representative) belongs in each of its two slots — a genuine ambiguity, disclosed rather than resolved.

fields.name: signatory2Surname
fields.label: Uzvārds
fields.description: Surname of the second signatory.

fields.name: signatory2PersonalCodeOrDateOfBirth
fields.label: Personas kods (ja nav personas koda, dzimšanas datums)
fields.description: The second signatory's Latvian personal code, or date of birth if they have no personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 9, second signature block — "Personas kods (ja nav personas koda, dzimšanas datums)"

fields.name: signatory2Signature
fields.label: Paraksts*
fields.description: The second signatory's printed name, standing in for their physical or electronic signature. The form's own footnote (marked with an asterisk) says this does not need to be filled in when the document is signed with a secure electronic signature bearing a qualified timestamp — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, disclosed rather than encoded.

fields.name: signatory2Date
fields.label: Datums*
fields.description: Date the second signatory signed the application. The form's own footnote (marked with an asterisk) says this does not need to be filled in when the document is signed with a secure electronic signature bearing a qualified timestamp — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, disclosed rather than encoded.

fields.name: stateFeePayerIsLegalPerson
fields.label: Juridiska persona
fields.description: Whether the state-fee payer is a legal person (Juridiska persona) rather than a natural person (Fiziska persona). Not itself a printed field — the form prints these as two alternate payer-identity sub-blocks, the same either/or convention as `hasLatvianPersonalCode` above. The form's own header says the whole of Section 10 is not filled in when the application is submitted via the Register of Enterprises' service portal (where the fee is payable only through the portal) or when a payment-confirming document is attached separately — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, so every Section 10 field is modelled unconditionally optional rather than encoded.

fields.name: stateFeePayerName
fields.label: Vārds
fields.description: Given name of the state-fee payer, when the payer is a natural person.

fields.name: stateFeePayerSurname
fields.label: Uzvārds
fields.description: Surname of the state-fee payer, when the payer is a natural person.

fields.name: stateFeePayerPersonalCodeOrDateOfBirth
fields.label: Personas kods (ja nav personas koda, norāda dzimšanas datumu, mēnesi, gadu)
fields.description: The natural-person state-fee payer's Latvian personal code, or date of birth if they have no personal code.
fields.sourceRef: Section 10, "Fiziska persona" block — "Personas kods (ja nav personas koda, norāda dzimšanas datumu, mēnesi, gadu)"

fields.name: stateFeePayerRegistrationNumber
fields.label: Reģistrācijas numurs
fields.description: Registration number of the state-fee payer, when the payer is a legal person.

fields.name: stateFeePayerLegalPersonName
fields.label: Nosaukums
fields.description: Name of the state-fee payer, when the payer is a legal person.

fields.name: paymentDate
fields.label: Maksājuma datums
fields.description: Date the state fee was paid. The form's own header says the whole of Section 10 is not filled in when the application is submitted via the Register of Enterprises' service portal (where the fee is payable only through the portal) or when a payment-confirming document is attached separately — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, so every Section 10 field is modelled unconditionally optional rather than encoded.

fields.name: paymentDocumentNumber
fields.label: Maksājuma dokumenta Nr.
fields.description: Number of the payment document, if any. Explicitly marked not obligatory on the form. The form's own header says the whole of Section 10 is not filled in when the application is submitted via the Register of Enterprises' service portal (where the fee is payable only through the portal) or when a payment-confirming document is attached separately — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, so every Section 10 field is modelled unconditionally optional rather than encoded.
fields.sourceRef: Section 10 — "Maksājuma dokumenta Nr. (Informācija nav norādāma obligāti)"

fields.name: paidAmount
fields.label: Apmaksātā summa
fields.description: Amount of the state fee paid, in euro. The form's own header says the whole of Section 10 is not filled in when the application is submitted via the Register of Enterprises' service portal (where the fee is payable only through the portal) or when a payment-confirming document is attached separately — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, so every Section 10 field is modelled unconditionally optional rather than encoded.

fields.name: paymentReferenceNumber
fields.label: Maksājuma references Nr.
fields.description: Payment reference number, if any. Explicitly marked not obligatory on the form. The form's own header says the whole of Section 10 is not filled in when the application is submitted via the Register of Enterprises' service portal (where the fee is payable only through the portal) or when a payment-confirming document is attached separately — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, so every Section 10 field is modelled unconditionally optional rather than encoded.
fields.sourceRef: Section 10 — "Maksājuma references Nr. (Informācija nav norādāma obligāti)"

fields.name: paymentServiceProviderName
fields.label: Maksājumu pakalpojuma sniedzēja nosaukums
fields.description: Name of the payment service provider (e.g. a bank), if any. Explicitly marked not obligatory on the form. The form's own header says the whole of Section 10 is not filled in when the application is submitted via the Register of Enterprises' service portal (where the fee is payable only through the portal) or when a payment-confirming document is attached separately — a condition with no corresponding field on the form, so every Section 10 field is modelled unconditionally optional rather than encoded.
fields.sourceRef: Section 10 — "Maksājumu pakalpojuma sniedzēja nosaukums (banka u.c.) (informācija nav norādāma obligāti)"

documents.label: Confirmation of reachability and lawful basis at the legal address
documents.statement: Apliecinu(-ām), ka tiesību subjekts ir sasniedzams un tam ir tiesisks pamats atrasties norādītajā juridiskajā adresē.
documents.sourceRef: Section 3, closing attestation line beneath the address table

documents.label: Confirmation of micro-enterprise tax payer eligibility
documents.statement: Apliecinu, ka: individuālais komersants nav mikrouzņēmuma nodokļa maksātājs un iedzīvotāju ienākuma nodokļa maksātājs no saimnieciskās darbības; paredzamais apgrozījums pārskata gadā, kas atbilst mikrouzņēmumu nodokļa taksācijas periodam, nepārsniegs pievienotās vērtības nodokļa reģistrācijas slieksni un netiek plānots kļūt par pievienotās vērtības nodokļa maksātāju līdz pievienotās vērtības nodokļa reģistrācijas slieksnim; mikrouzņēmums vēlas maksāt mikrouzņēmumu nodokli.
documents.sourceRef: Section 4, "Apliecinājums" block beneath the opt-in checkbox
